bugfix_gate_complete: qa-spec -> dev -> qa-validate PASS_WITH_NOTES -> USER INTERACTIVE RE-TEST (the manual-TTY gate). User confirmed on real binary: Ctrl+C no longer exits (does-nothing = spec-correct, AC only requires interrupt 'while processing' which is sync in M1; user accepted as-is, ship). Ctrl+D clean exit, NO 'd' flash (race didn't materialize). --debug streams [debug] token_issued/session_start/preflight_called to stderr. User: 'looks good'.
section7_done_definition_ALL_PASS: Verified by 2 user interactive sessions + file inspection: (1) --version OK (2) teo> prompt OK (3) mechanical+stub OK (4) architectural+stub OK (5) blorp->architectural no error OK (6) Ctrl+D exit0 OK (7) history file <route>:<text>, UNKNOWN written architectural: (2+2/test a thing/add some numbers all architectural:) OK (8) --debug shows token issuance + per-submit preflight OK. ALL 8 PASS = M1 SHIPS.
ctrlc_ux_decision: User accepted Ctrl+C no-op as spec-correct for M1. M2 follow-up: revisit Ctrl+C visible-feedback UX alongside async-interrupt (when there's real processing to interrupt).
manual_tty_todos_closed: Ctrl+C (returns/no-exit), Ctrl+D (clean exit no flash), --debug (stderr stream) all verified by user in real TTY. The 3 it.todo manual cases in keys.test.tsx are now human-verified; record in go-signal.
classifier_DEFERRED_to_M2: User chose 2026-05-29: defer classifier expansion to M2. M1 ships with sparse patterns (smoke passes, blorp the fleeb routes correctly). SE's GROUND-TRUTH PRINCIPLE to encode in M2 (DO NOT example-fit): mechanical = single deterministic op ('just do it' is complete instruction); architectural = requires judgment ('it depends' is legit); unknown = genuinely ambiguous. Per principle: arithmetic/compute (2+2, add 2+2, add some numbers, calculate/compute/convert/solve) = MECHANICAL (gap: no numeric/compute patterns exist); 'test a thing' = stays UNKNOWN->architectural (correct). UNKNOWN->architectural display UNCHANGED (PM-locked, SE confirmed correct, no PM escalation). M2 work order: qa writes tests-from-principle FIRST, then dev expands patterns, SE reviews. D-005 (ground-truth principle) to be authored WHEN M2 work happens, not now.
bugfix_gate1_complete: qa-spec done. New tests/repl/keys.test.tsx (13 cases, 3 manual-TTY todos). 5 red for right reasons. Relabeled misleading T-13/T-14 + golden Step2/6 with fidelity notes (piped-EOF != interactive Ctrl+D). Suite 190 pass/5 fail/7 todo. Dev contracts: Ctrl+C = exitOnCtrlC:false in render() + useInput handler clears input NOT exit; Ctrl+D = useInput key.ctrl&&input==='d' -> exit()+process.exit(0), KEEP stdin.on('end'); --debug = add process.stderr.write for token_issued/preflight_called/matched_pattern. Real-PTY versions are it.todo manual (user re-tests).
ci_green_confirmed: CI GREEN on 85e4769 (run 26655241660): Lint, Typecheck, Build x2, Test x2, Smoke x2, Gate all pass. First green run. Step 14 done. (annotations = cache hiccups + Node20 deprecation warning, non-blocking).
interactive_session_defects: User ran real TTY session, found 4 defects the scripted pass + 185 green tests MISSED: (1) Ctrl+C CLOSES repl (should return to prompt, T-13) — App.tsx has NO useInput Ctrl+C handler, falls to Ink default terminate. (2) Ctrl+D does NOT exit, inserts 'd' into TextInput (should exit 0, T-14) — App.tsx only handles stdin.on('end') which fires on piped EOF but NOT interactive Ctrl+D keypress in raw mode. (3) --debug shows NOTHING visible — dev wired writeAuditEvent (file only), spec Section 5 says debug should ALSO stream token issuance/preflight/matched-pattern to STDERR. (4) classifier ground-truth weak: 'test a thing','add some numbers','add 2 + 2','2+2' ALL fell through to UNKNOWN->architectural (add pattern requires 'add the \w'; arithmetic matches nothing). TEST FIDELITY FAILURE: T-13/T-14 green via subprocess-EOF + component sim, neither exercises real interactive raw-mode key events.
fix_scope_decision: User chose: 2 clear bugs (Ctrl+C/Ctrl+D key handling + --debug stderr stream) -> FIX cycle now. Classifier ground-truth -> SEPARATE design decision (staff-eng owns seed patterns, possibly PM on uncertain-classification UX). Do NOT bolt regexes onto the 4 examples.

bug_fix_tracks.qa-spec-fix: Write failing tests for Ctrl+C return-to-prompt, Ctrl+D clean exit, --debug stderr stream. CONFRONT why T-13/T-14 passed while binary broke (fidelity gap) — pin contract as tightly as possible w/o real PTY; mark genuinely-PTY-only parts as manual.
bug_fix_tracks.classifier-design: staff-eng decides ground-truth: what SHOULD route mechanical vs architectural vs UNKNOWN; whether arithmetic/'add X' are mechanical; the uncertain-classification UX (everything->architectural is current, is that right?). Design decision, not example-fitting.

nontty_test_ready: qa wrote tests/integration/non-tty.test.ts. 1 red (piped stdin exits 0 silently, should exit non-zero w/ message), 2 green regression guards (--version/--help survive), 1 todo (interactive TTY = manual gate). Contract: guard in index.tsx AFTER parseArgs, BEFORE render(): if(!process.stdin.isTTY){stderr 'interactive terminal required'; exit(1)}. Suite 184 pass/1 red/4 todo.
phase_2b_fixes_done: All 6 dev fixes landed: T-49 fatal exit (a/b/c/d green), keys.ts excluded+ignore-removed, App.tsx manufactured import removed, M2+ comment, index.tsx TEO_FORCE_STARTUP_ERROR hook. Suite 182 pass/3 todo/0 fail, global gate PASS, zero ignore directives in src/, typecheck clean. Binary rebuilt 11:10. D-004 authored.
scripted_live_pass_finding: DEFECT found by Sage scripted live pass on REAL compiled binary (dist/teo-darwin-arm64). --version=0.1.0 OK, --help OK. BUT piped/non-interactive stdin: NO output, NO history file, NO audit log, exits 0 SILENTLY. Whole submit pipeline (classify->route->history->audit) dead on piped input. Root cause: ink-text-input needs raw TTY mode; piped stdin has no TTY so onSubmit never fires. 182 green tests MISS this: golden 'REPL flow' steps 3/4/5/8 use ink-testing-library component sim, never pipe into real binary. Only binary-subprocess steps 1/2/6 (--version/EOF/Ctrl+D) run against real binary. Exit code on piped input = 0 (clean, not error) = the bad part (silent lie of success).
nontty_decision: User chose: route back to dev (treat silent no-op as real defect). Behavior = Option (a) DEFAULT unless user overrides: detect non-TTY stdin -> print 'teo requires an interactive terminal' -> exit NON-ZERO. NOT batch mode (that's unspecified M1 feature scope). Follows CAD: qa writes failing binary-level subprocess test FIRST, then dev implements.
